meta.name: IGDS-SF9 Internet Gaming Disorder Scale
meta.fullName: Internet Gaming Disorder Scale - Short Form 9
meta.description: The IGDS-SF9 is a 9-item brief self-report scale based on DSM-5 Internet Gaming Disorder criteria for assessing the severity of gaming addiction.
meta.author: Pontes & Griffiths (2015)
meta.reference: Pontes HM, Griffiths MD. Measuring DSM-5 internet gaming disorder: Development and validation of a short psychometric scale. Computers in Human Behavior. 2015;45:137-143.
meta.instruction: Based on your gaming behavior over the past 12 months, select the option that best describes your experience.

questions.text: I feel preoccupied with my gaming behavior (e.g., thinking about previous gaming activity, planning the next session, thinking of ways to get more game currency)
I feel more irritability, anxiety, or even sadness when I try to either reduce or stop my gaming activity
I feel the need to spend increasing amount of time engaged in gaming to achieve satisfaction or pleasure
I systematically fail when trying to control or cease my gaming activity
I have lost interest in previous hobbies and other entertainment activities as a result of my engagement with gaming
I have continued my gaming activity despite knowing it was causing problems between me and other people
I have deceived any of my family members, therapists, or others about the amount of my gaming activity
I play in order to temporarily escape or relieve a negative mood (e.g., helplessness, guilt, anxiety)
I have jeopardized or lost an important relationship, job, or educational/career opportunity because of my gaming activity

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your gaming behavior falls within the normal range with no indicators of Internet Gaming Disorder. Gaming appears to be a healthy recreational activity that does not interfere with your responsibilities or well-being.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy and balanced gaming habits alongside other life activities
Stay aware of your gaming time and its impact on sleep, work, and social relationships
Model healthy gaming behavior for peers and younger family members in your environment

ranges.label: At Risk
ranges.description: You show some patterns of problematic gaming behavior that place you at risk for developing Internet Gaming Disorder. Gaming may occasionally interfere with sleep, responsibilities, or social engagement.
suggestions: Set clear daily time limits for gaming sessions and use built-in parental or timer controls
Actively develop and invest in hobbies and social activities that do not involve gaming
Monitor the impact of gaming on your sleep quality, academic or work performance, and relationships
If you find it increasingly difficult to control gaming time, consider consulting a professional early

ranges.label: High Risk
ranges.description: Your gaming behavior has noticeably impacted multiple areas of daily life including social relationships, academic or occupational performance, and physical health. Professional guidance is recommended at this level.
suggestions: Consulting a mental health professional experienced with behavioral addictions is recommended
Gradually reduce gaming time using a structured schedule rather than attempting abrupt cessation
Identify and develop alternative sources of relaxation, achievement, and social connection offline
Openly discuss your gaming habits with someone you trust for external perspective and accountability
Address underlying emotional needs that gaming may be fulfilling, such as stress relief or escapism

ranges.label: Probable Gaming Disorder
ranges.description: Your score suggests probable Internet Gaming Disorder. Gaming has severely affected your daily functioning, relationships, and well-being, meeting criteria that warrant immediate professional clinical attention.
suggestions: Seeking evaluation from a psychiatrist or psychotherapist specializing in gaming addiction is urgent
Professional behavioral intervention therapy can effectively address compulsive gaming patterns
Build a support system by informing family and close friends about the severity of the situation
Consider environmental controls such as limiting unsupervised access to gaming devices and accounts
